Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix RAG retrieval: always combine vector search + direct DB queries

retrieveKnowledge() had mutually exclusive RAG/FTS paths — when RAG
was available, direct DB queries for websites/partnerships were skipped
entirely, causing the chatbot to miss website content, courses, coupons.

Now runs direct DB queries (websites, coupons, partnerships, insights)
in parallel with RAG vector search, merging results. Also increases
website content limits (4→10 pages, 500→2000 chars) and instructs the
LLM to explain content naturally instead of quoting verbatim.

chore: fix changelog version to v0.2.5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Restore GOAT /oʊ/ diphthong glide in en-us

GOAT was monophthongized (oʊ→oː) to prevent drawn-out glides on
polysyllabic words like "social" and "combobox", but this killed the
glide entirely on monosyllabic "no", "go", "home" — sounding flat and
foreign (ref #38). Remove the monophthongization rule and use
pairScale 0.85 instead, giving GOAT a real but short glide that works
for both cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(statusline): embed cooldown into cache JSON

Instead of a separate .cooldown file, store _cooldown_until timestamp
directly in the cache JSON. On successful API response the field is
naturally replaced (API data doesn't contain it). Providers check for
"five_hour"/"seven_day" key presence to distinguish real data from
cooldown-only cache.

commit.message:
Fix Supabase admin client leaking auth refresh timers (#56)

createServerClient() created new clients with default auth options
(auto-refresh timers, session persistence) on every call. In
chart-cache.ts loops this accumulated background timers causing memory
leaks. Switch to the existing createAdminClient() which properly
disables autoRefreshToken and persistSession, and remove the redundant
createServerClient factory.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Simplify AI command discovery

- Add ProAgents commands section to README.md during init
- AI tools auto-read README.md, so commands are discovered automatically
- Simplify PROAGENTS.md to essential commands only
- Remove need for user to explain ProAgents to AI
- User just types /help and it works

How it works:
1. proagents init → adds commands to README.md
2. AI reads README.md automatically
3. User types /help, /fix, /status etc.
4. Commands work without any setup

commit.message:
feat: auto-provisioning via Clerk Invitation (público 50+)

Webhook Hotmart v2.1.0:
- Caso A: buyer já tem conta Clerk → cria tenant+profile+licença direto
- Caso B: buyer sem conta → envia Clerk Invitation (email com link
  "Crie sua conta") + pre-cria tenant/profile pendente + licença
- Profile pendente usa clerk_user_id='pending_hotmart_{email}'
- No primeiro login, /api/user/tenant detecta e ativa automaticamente

/api/user/tenant atualizado:
- Quando ativa profile pendente (pending_*), também atualiza
  clerk_user_id no tenant e user_licenses

Sem "esqueci senha" — email claro com link para criar conta.

commit.message:
fix: correct stream-json result event parsing

StreamEvent.Result was typed as *StreamResult (object) but Claude emits
"result" as a plain string. JSON unmarshal silently failed, leaving
FullOutput always empty and warden verdict parsing always broken.

Actual format: {"type":"result","result":"<text>","total_cost_usd":0.12,"usage":{...}}

- Replace StreamResult with StreamUsage (just token counts)
- StreamEvent.Result is now string, TotalCostUSD float64, Usage *StreamUsage
- Capture FullOutput/CostUSD/tokens from type=="result" event

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(pipeline): filter generic tags in Jaccard computation to prevent disk exhaustion

Tags like 'jazz' (17K artists) or 'rock' (16K) produce ~160M pairs each in the
self-join before GROUP BY, filling the 75GB disk. Fix: join tag_stats and exclude
tags with artist_count > 2000. Also bumps work_mem to 256MB for the session.

- harness-pipeline.yaml: new 4-stage Harness pipeline
  Stage 1: Validate inputs (projectId format, required fields, email)
  Stage 2: Native HarnessApproval gate (platform_admins group, 1d timeout)
  Stage 3: Call Jenkins endpoint (on approval) + send approval email
  Stage 4: Send SMTP rejection email (on rejection)

Jenkins job: idp-repo-okta-teams-provisioner
  Receives all parameters to provision repo, Okta groups, GitHub teams

Required secrets: jenkins_url, jenkins_user, jenkins_api_token,
  smtp_host, smtp_port, smtp_user, smtp_password, smtp_from_address

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix realized P&L using FIFO cost basis instead of global weighted average (#52) (#54)

The trade matcher was calculating realized P&L using a global weighted
average of all entries, causing scale-in/out trades to have incorrect
P&L that changed retroactively when new shares were added. Now computes
P&L incrementally in reducePosition using per-lot FIFO cost basis with
prorated commissions. Also fixes break-even trades (P&L=0) returning
null due to truthy check, and monthly stats treating null P&L as zero.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(income): reconhece todas as seções de meses em PDFs mesclados

Problema: PDFs mesclados de 6 meses usam cabeçalhos como "Setembro 2025"
(só mês+ano, sem dia). Sem uma data DD/MM reconhecível, o parser perdia o
contexto de data para aquelas seções — transações não eram associadas a
nenhum mês e ficavam invisíveis.

Correções:
- Pre-normalização "Setembro 2025" / "setembro/2025" → "01/SET/2025": cabeçalhos
  de seção sem dia viram datas sintéticas que ativam o dataContextual correto
- anoContextual: rastreia o último ano 4-dígitos visto; datas DD/MM sem ano
  usam esse ano em vez de new Date().getFullYear() (que daria 2026 para
  extratos de 2025)
- DATA_RE: separador de ponto (DD.MM.YYYY) suportado via [/\-\.\s]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

why: resolved findings were lost after each reconciliation — archiving them to .dev/learnings/ builds searchable institutional memory that compounds across sessions

commit.message:
Fix IDOR: scope DELETE account link to its connection (#53)

The DELETE handler parsed connectionId from the URL but never used it,
allowing any broker_account_link to be deleted via any connectionId.
Now validates connection exists and filters the delete by both link ID
and broker_connection_id.

commit.message:
Add multi-distro installer support (apt/dnf/pacman/zypper)

Addresses issue #33 (CachyOS) and enables the installer to work on
Fedora, Arch-based, and openSUSE distros in addition to Ubuntu/Debian.

- Add packaging.sh library for distro-agnostic package management
- Convert Phase 05 (Docker/NVIDIA toolkit) to multi-distro case blocks
- Convert Phase 01, 07, 10 error messages and install hints
- Add 6-distro CI matrix (ubuntu, debian, fedora, arch, opensuse)
- Add Distrobox-based multi-distro test runner
- Add TESTING.md documenting Ventoy + Distrobox workflow

Note: Phase 02 (NVIDIA driver install) and detection.sh (Secure Boot)
still use apt — these require distro-specific driver packages and
verification commands that need dedicated Ventoy testing per distro.

commit.message:
Rerecord wizard sessions 321-333 with full event/cursor/mapdump data

All 13 wizard gameplay sessions (seed321-seed333) have been rerecorded
using the current C harness, adding:
- Event entries (^place, ^makemon, ^die, etc.) embedded in rng arrays
- Cursor tracking data per step [col, row, visible]
- Map dump checkpoints and typgrid data

The old wizard sessions had 0 ^event entries, making event comparisons
trivially pass (0/0). With 800-15000+ events per session now captured,
pre-existing JS parity bugs are now visible for triage.

Two known regressions accepted (their bugs now visible and debuggable):
- seed321: topologize() roomno divergence on dungeon level 21 (step 193)
- seed329: horizontalGrid divergence on level 24 + cursor at step 222

All rerecorded sessions have regen.raw_moves=True set to ensure future
rerecord.py invocations use the correct --raw-moves path.

Also:
- pes-report.mjs: auto-detects staleness by comparing git note timestamp
  against newest mtime of session files, js/, and test/comparison/ dirs;
  auto-reruns tests instead of serving stale cached results (--cached flag
  forces git note reading without staleness check)
- docs/SESSION_FORMAT_V3.md: added cursor field docs; clarified that rng
  arrays contain mixed RNG calls, midlog markers, and event entries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fixes #60
